model_status: June 2 SharePoint/OpenRocket geometry with June 14 M5 launch conditions; mass properties and aerodynamic calibration remain unresolved

provenance.report_source: Project AMBAR M5 Report, Milestone 5 Critical Design Evaluation, dated June 14, 2026
provenance.rocket_geometry: June 2 SharePoint OpenRocket file: 0.039624 m outer radius, 0.28575 m nose, 1.49225 m total length, and current stabilizing-fin placement
provenance.launch_site: M5 initial-launch-condition figure: 27.93469 deg, -80.70953 deg, 23 ft elevation
provenance.launch_rail: M5 initial-launch-condition figure: 72 in rail and 0 deg from vertical
provenance.wind: M5 initial-launch-condition figure: 3.58 m/s average from 225 deg; nominal run applies a constant horizontal wind and Monte Carlo dispersion remains separate
provenance.stabilizing_fins: M5 fin-flutter tables: 3 fins, 6 in root chord, 2 in tip chord, 3 in span, 0.125 in thickness
provenance.motor: M5 report decision matrix: AeroTech J420R; thrust curve from ThrustCurve.org certified public-domain RASP data
provenance.motor_burn_time: J420R thrust-curve endpoint: 1.64 s; deployment is gated for an additional 0.10 s
provenance.passive_apogee: June 14 M5 requirement-validation table reports 3379 ft; the June 11 full-report copy contains an older 4005 ft design result
provenance.airbrake_load: M5 drag table reports 54.8215 N per fin and 219.286 N for four airbrake fins at 197.206 m/s
provenance.dry_mass: PLACEHOLDER - replace with measured flight-ready mass
provenance.inertia_and_center_of_mass: PLACEHOLDER - replace with CAD/OpenRocket mass properties
provenance.rocket_drag: PLACEHOLDER - calibrate from the final OpenRocket drag-vs-Mach export
provenance.airbrake_drag: PLACEHOLDER - replace with CFD/wind-tunnel drag coefficient vs Mach and deployment
provenance.sensor_noise: PROVISIONAL - LSM6DSV32X +/-32 g resolution is datasheet-backed; bias, white noise, barometer error, and latency require bench calibration
provenance.rail_buttons: PLACEHOLDER - positions retained from the earlier RocketPy model until measured from the current assembly
